profile.name: Saidamir Bobojonov
profile.title: Python Backend Engineer
profile.summary: Backend-focused software engineer with experience building production-grade systems in Python (Django, DRF, FastAPI) and Node.js, with hands-on expertise in microservices architecture, Docker, Kubernetes, Kafka, and Redis.

experience.title: Backend Engineer
highlights: Architected and delivered a production-grade LMS (Learning Management System) for employee training and logistics workflows, decomposed into 5+ independently deployable microservices using Node.js and Django REST Framework — containerized with Docker and orchestrated via Kubernetes.
Integrated Apache Kafka for asynchronous inter-service messaging, decoupling notification, event-processing, and training-progress pipelines from core application services.
Built Google Drive API integration to automate document management workflows — enabling automatic file organization, access control, and reporting without manual intervention.
Designed and optimized PostgreSQL schemas for multi-tenant user and course data, implementing indexing strategies that reduced average query latency by ~30%.
Configured Nginx reverse proxy with Gunicorn workers and S3/MinIO object storage for media and content asset management.

experience.company: Cultural & Historical Complex of Khujand Fortress
experience.title: Software Developer & Technical Project Coordinator
highlights: Designed, developed, and maintained the official institutional website using Django and Python, including backend logic, PostgreSQL database schema design, admin panel customization, and dynamic content management.
Built internal utility tools including a QR code generation system used for exhibit labeling and visitor information — reducing manual coordination effort across departments.
Managed website content administration and ensured consistent uptime for a live public-facing production environment.
Authored technical sections of grant proposals for international cultural preservation programs, contributing to successful funding applications from EU and CIS-region bodies.

experience.company: Federal Institute for Materials Research and Testing (BAM)
experience.title: Python Programmer Intern
highlights: Developed Python automation scripts and ML (machine learning) models using scikit-learn to detect structural defects and classify material properties — improving defect identification accuracy by ~40% over prior manual baselines.
Engineered end-to-end data ingestion and preprocessing pipelines in Python, reducing analyst data preparation time by 45% and enabling faster model training and validation cycles.
Collaborated with a cross-functional team of materials scientists and engineers to integrate automated workflows into existing laboratory systems.
Recognized with an official Certified Internship Python Programmer credential issued by BAM Berlin (2024).

experience.title: Software Developer & Technical Project Coordinator
highlights: Developed and maintained multiple institutional websites using Django and Python, implementing CMS features, admin tooling, and dynamic content management — including pulatov-school.tj and tourismcenter.tj (both live in production).
Built internal utility tools and automation scripts to streamline university operations, improving staff workflows and reducing manual administrative overhead.

Authored technical sections of grant proposals for international programs (EU, CIS-region funding bodies), contributing to successful applications for university digitization and innovation initiatives.
Provided full-stack technical support across web infrastructure, internal university systems, and staff-facing tooling.
Collaborated with faculty and administrative teams to scope, plan, and deliver software projects aligned with institutional goals.

projects.name: LMS Microservices Platform
projects.description: Built a full multi-tenant LMS for a U.S. logistics company, decomposed into independently deployable microservices. Includes auth, course management, content delivery, real-time notifications via Kafka, Google Drive automation, and admin dashboard. Deployed on Kubernetes.

projects.name: Materials Defect Detection System
projects.description: Developed ML-based defect classification and Python automation pipelines for BAM Berlin research lab, achieving ~40% accuracy improvement over manual baselines in structural materials testing.

projects.name: pulatov-school.tj & tourismcenter.tj
projects.description: Developed and deployed two live production websites — a school platform and a tourism center portal — including CMS, admin tooling, QR tools, and dynamic content management.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science, Software and Computer Science

certifications.name: Certified Internship Python Programmer
certifications.issuer: Federal Institute for Materials Research and Testing BAM, Berlin
